The best immediate progress is to push the box up onto the target. Since the player is already below/right? Actually to push up, the player must be below the box, which is not the case. But from current adjacency, the legal productive push is push left? No. Re-evaluate: player at (2,2), box at (2,1), so player is to the right of box. A push left moves the box from (2,1) to (2,0), blocked by wall. So no push possible there. Best is to reposition around the left box to get below it and push up to target, but accessing (3,1) is blocked by the wall at (3,2) yet (3,1) itself is open via moving down then left. From (2,2), moving down to (3,2) is blocked, up to (1,2) is open, left is occupied by box, right is open. To reach (3,1), path can be (2,2)->(2,3)->(2,2)? No, need route via lower corridor: right, down blocked at (3,3)? open, then left to (3,1) through (3,3)->(4,3)->(4,2)? target open->(4,1)->(3,1). Immediate first step should be right to begin circling around and stop oscillating near the box. Also this opens path toward box at (3,4) if needed.
